title: Copy the value in a mirrored column

posts.author: silverbackdollar (Miles Lunde)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m trying to copy a number in a mirrored column to another column the first time a status changes to something.

For context, we have resource’s rates which I want to lock in while doing some costings. The problem I’m having is when I look back on this item in 6 months and the resource’s rate has now changed, it changes all the historical calculations and I can’t see what their rate was back then.

Here’s the formula I have now: IF({Final Cost Locked} = “Final Costs Entered”, {Subitems CPM Cost}, “Not Finalised”)

But of course the problem is that, so long as the status column “Final Cost Locked” = “Final Costs Entered”, it will keep updating the value to whatever the mirrored column currently displays.

I can’t for the life of me figure out how to accomplish this. Does anyone have an idea?

posts.author: peta (Peta Bolger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @silverbackdollar

Maybe check out something like Column Magic Copy to help you along. You can copy the value of a mirror column based on a status trigger and paste it into a local number column or text column:

image940×151 37.4 KB

Hope that’s helpful - let me know if you need further detail.
peta@upstreamtech.io

posts.author: silverbackdollar (Miles Lunde)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Peta - I think that would do the trick. Thanks for the suggestion!

posts.author: Sara413 (Sara Mahan)
posts.cookedHtml: hi @peta I attempted to use this recipe and instead of copying the sum of the cell which is 25, it’s just returning the number one (1)…what am I doing wrong?

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

If you’re looking to copy mirror column you can use our Column to Column app.

image1332×290 11.6 KB

It supports coping mirror column to local column, copying from local column to mirror column and many more (including formula columns, mirror triggers, …)

image1507×821 73.1 KB

posts.author: hellotimi (David Kalu (Formula Pro - Use formula column results in automations!))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @silverbackdollar,

I saw your question about needing to copy the value from a mirrored column into another column (like a numbers column to ‘lock in’ a rate). That’s a super common and frustrating limitation with monday.com’s native mirror columns – they’re great for display, but not always for action or historical data.

We’ve actually developed a direct solution for this with our Formula PRO app. We just published a detailed guide on how you can use it to automatically copy those mirrored values to any native column you need, which then makes them fully usable for calculations, automations, or just keeping a static record.

You can find the full tutorial and setup steps here: Copy the value in a mirrored column

Hope this helps!

Let me know if you have any questions after reading!
